query:
You are working on the ankidroid/Anki-Android repository.
The codebase is at version 2.173 (commit: 93f4312a).

## Issue to Resolve

Add `Show play buttons on cards with audio` preference as in Anki Desktop
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
Anki Desktop has a feature called  `Show play buttons on cards with audio`, but AnkiDroid doesn't have it

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Add the pref to AnkiDroid


**Additional context**
![image](https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/assets/69634269/37388d7f-7313-4ce6-86f9-9f04d228dddf)

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aankitects%2Fanki%20hide_audio_play_buttons&type=code

https://github.com/ankitects/anki/blob/e327195470824074465657f98bb0282c7c93a56e/qt/aqt/main.py#L587-L592

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/AbstractFlashcardViewer.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/analytics/UsageAnalytics.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/cardviewer/HtmlGenerator.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/preferences/AppearanceSettingsFragment.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/SoundKt.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/res/values/preferences.xml`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/res/xml/preferences_appearance.xml`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

See the GNU General Public License for more details. 12→ * 13→ * You should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License along with 14→ * this program. If not, see . 15→ * 16→ * This file incorporates code under the following license 17→ * https://github.com/ankitects/anki/blob/2.1.34/pylib/anki/sound.py 18→ * 19→ * Copyright: Ankitects Pty Ltd and contributors 20→ * License: GNU AGPL, version 3 or later; http://www.gnu.org/licenses/agpl.html 21→ */ 22→ 23→@file:Suppress("unused") 24→ 25→package com.ichi2.libanki 26→ 27→import com.ichi2.anki.CollectionManager.withCol 28→import com.ichi2.utils.KotlinCleanup 29→ 30→@KotlinCleanup("combine file with Sound.kt if done in libAnki") 31→/** 32→ * Records information about a text to speech tag. 33→ */ 34→data class TTSTag( 35→ val fieldText: String, 36→ /** 37→ * Language may be empty if coming from AnkiDroid reading the whole card 38→ */ 39→ val lang: String, 40→ val voices: List, 41→ val speed: Float, 42→ /** each arg should be in the form 'foo=bar' */ 43→ val otherArgs: List 44→) : AvTag() 45→ 46→/** 47→ * Contains the filename inside a [sound:...] tag. 48→ * 49→ * Video files also use [sound:...]. 50→ */ 51→data class SoundOrVideoTag(val filename: String) : AvTag() 52→ 53→/** In python, this is a union of [TTSTag] and [SoundOrVideoTag] */ 54→open class AvTag 55→ 56→/* Methods */ 57→ 58→val AV_REF_RE = Regex("\\[anki:(play:(.):(\\d+))]") 59→val AV_PLAYLINK_RE = Regex("playsound:(.):(\\d+)") 60→ 61→fun stripAvRefs(text: String) = AV_REF_RE.replace("", text) 62→ 63→fun addPlayIcons(content: String): String { 64→ return AV_REF_RE.replace(content) { match -> 65→ val groups = match.groupValues 66→ val side = groups[2] 67→ val index = groups[3] 68→ val playsound = "playsound:$side:$index" 69→ """ 70→ 72→ """ 73→ } 74→} 75→ 76→/** Extract av tag from playsound:q:x link */ 77→suspend fun getAvTag(card: Card, url: String): AvTag? { 78→ return AV_PLAYLINK_RE.matchEntire(url)?.let { 79→ val values = it.groupValues 80→ val questionSide = values[1] == "q" 81→ val index = values[2].toInt() 82→ val tags = withCol { 83→ if (questionSide) { card.questionAvTags() } else { card.answerAvTags() } 84→ } 85→ if (index < tags.size) { 86→ tags[index] 87→ } else { 88→ null 89→ } 90→ } 91→} 92→

1→/****************************************************************************************
  2→ * Copyright (c) 2009 Edu Zamora <edu.zasu@gmail.com>                                   *

 17→
 18→package com.ichi2.libanki
 19→
 20→import android.content.Context
 21→import android.media.*
 22→import android.media.AudioManager.OnAudioFocusChangeListener
 23→import android.media.MediaPlayer.OnCompletionListener
 24→import android.net.Uri
 25→import android.os.Build
 26→import androidx.annotation.CheckResult
 27→import androidx.annotation.VisibleForTesting
 28→import com.ichi2.anki.AnkiDroidApp
 29→import com.ichi2.anki.ReadText
 30→import com.ichi2.compat.CompatHelper
 31→import com.ichi2.libanki.Sound.OnErrorListener.ErrorHandling.CONTINUE_AUDIO
 32→import com.ichi2.libanki.Sound.SoundSide.*
 33→import timber.log.Timber
 34→import java.util.*
 35→import java.util.regex.Pattern
 36→
 37→val VIDEO_EXTENSIONS = setOf("mp4", "mov", "mpg", "mpeg", "mkv", "avi")
 38→
 39→private typealias SoundPath = String
 40→// NICE_TO_HAVE: Abstract, then add tests for #6111
 41→/**
 42→ * Parses, loads and plays sound & video files
 43→ * Called `Sound` Anki uses `[sound:]` for both audio and video
 44→ */
 45→class Sound(private val soundPlayer: SoundPlayer, private val soundDir: String) : SoundPlayer() {
 46→    /**
 47→     * @param soundDir base path to the media files
 48→     */
 49→    constructor(soundDir: String) : this(SoundPlayer(), soundDir)
 50→
 51→    /**
 52→     * The subset of sounds to involve
 53→     * @param int Used for serialisation
 54→     */
 55→    enum class SoundSide(val int: Int) {
 56→        QUESTION(0), ANSWER(1), QUESTION_AND_ANSWER(2);
 57→    }
 58→
 59→    /** Sounds for the question/answer of a card */
 60→    // Stops code paths where QUESTION_AND_ANSWER is invalid
 61→    enum class SingleSoundSide {
 62→        QUESTION, ANSWER;
 63→
 64→        fun toSoundSide(): SoundSide = when (this) {
 65→            QUESTION -> SoundSide.QUESTION
 66→            ANSWER -> SoundSide.ANSWER
 67→        }
 68→    }
 69→
 70→    /**
 71→     * Stores sounds for the current card. Maps from a side to paths paths
 72→     * Should be accessed via [getSounds]
 73→     */
 74→    private val soundPaths: MutableMap<SoundSide, MutableList<SoundPath>> = EnumMap(SoundSide::class.java)
 75→
 76→    /** Returns a non-empty list of sounds, or null if there are no values */
 77→    @VisibleForTesting
 78→    fun getSounds(side: SoundSide) = getSoundList(side).let {
 79→        if (!it.any()) null else it
 80→    }
 81→
 82→    private fun getSoundList(side: SoundSide): List<SoundPath> {
 83→        if (side == QUESTION_AND_ANSWER) {
 84→            return getSoundList(QUESTION) + getSoundList(ANSWER)
 85→        }
 86→        return soundPaths[side] ?: emptyList()
 87→    }
 88→
 89→    /**
 90→     * Clears current sound paths; call before [expandSounds] + [addSounds]
 91→     * is called for the next card
 92→     */
 93→    fun resetSounds() {
 94→        soundPaths.clear()
 95→    }
 96→
 97→    /**
 98→     * Stores entries to the filepaths for sounds, categorized as belonging to the front (question) or back (answer) of cards.
 99→     * Note that all sounds embedded in the content will be given the same base categorization of question or answer.
100→     * Additionally, the result is to be sorted by the order of appearance on the card.
101→     * @param tags the entries expected in display order
102→     * @param side the base categorization of the sounds in the content
103→     */
104→    fun addSounds(tags: List<SoundOrVideoTag>, side: SingleSoundSide) {
105→        val soundPathCollection = soundPaths.getOrPut(side.toSoundSide()) { mutableListOf() }
106→
107→        Timber.d("Adding %d sounds to side: %s", tags.size, side)
108→        val paths = tags.map { getSoundPath(soundDir, it.filename) }
109→        soundPathCollection.addAll(paths)
110→    }
111→
112→    /** Plays all the sounds for the indicated side(s)  */
113→    fun playSounds(side: SoundSide, errorListener: OnErrorListener?) {
114→        // If there are sounds to play for the current card, start with the first one
115→        val soundPaths = getSounds(side) ?: return
116→        Timber.d("playSounds: playing $side")
117→        this.playSound(
118→            soundPaths[0],
119→            PlayAllCompletionListener(side, errorListener),
120→            errorListener
121→        )
122→    }
123→
124→    /** Returns the total length of all sounds for the side in milliseconds */
125→    fun getSoundsLength(side: SoundSide): Long {
126→        val soundPaths = getSounds(side) ?: return 0
127→        val metaRetriever = MediaMetadataRetriever()
128→        val context = AnkiDroidApp.instance.applicationContext
129→        return soundPaths
130→            .map { Uri.parse(it) }
131→            .sumOf { uri ->
132→                try {
133→                    metaRetriever.getDuration(context, uri)
134→                } catch (e: Exception) {
135→                    Timber.w(
136→                        e,
137→                        "metaRetriever - Error setting Data Source for mediaRetriever (media doesn't exist or forbidden?)."
138→                    )
139→                    0
140→                }
141→            }
142→    }
143→
144→    /**
145→     * Class used to play all sounds for a given card side
146→     */
147→    private inner class PlayAllCompletionListener(
148→        private val side: SoundSide,
149→        private val errorListener: OnErrorListener?
150→    ) : OnCompletionListener {
151→        /** Index of next sound to play inside `getSounds(side)` */
152→        // this is a completion listener: [onCompletion] is first called after the first sound
153→        private var nextIndexToPlay = 1
154→        override fun onCompletion(mp: MediaPlayer) {
155→            val paths = getSounds(side) ?: emptyList() // emptyList -> stopSounds()
156→            // If there are still more sounds to play for the current card, play the next one
157→            if (nextIndexToPlay < paths.size) {
158→                Timber.i("Play all: Playing next sound")
159→                playSound(paths[nextIndexToPlay++], this, errorListener)
160→            } else {
161→                Timber.i("Play all: Completed - releasing sound")
162→                soundPlayer.stopSounds()
163→            }
164→        }
165→    }
166→
167→    override fun stopSounds() {
168→        soundPlayer.stopSounds()
169→        ReadText.stopTts() // TODO: Reconsider design
170→    }
171→
172→    fun hasQuestion(): Boolean = getSounds(QUESTION) != null
173→
174→    fun hasAnswer(): Boolean = getSounds(ANSWER) != null
175→
176→    fun interface OnErrorListener {
177→        fun onError(mp: MediaPlayer?, which: Int, extra: Int, path: String?): ErrorHandling
178→
179→        enum class ErrorHandling {
180→            /** Stop playing audio */
181→            STOP_AUDIO,
182→
183→            /** Continue to the next audio (if any) */
184→            CONTINUE_AUDIO,
185→
186→            /** Retry the current audio */
187→            RETRY_AUDIO
188→        }
189→    }
190→
191→    companion object {
192→        /**
193→         * Pattern used to identify the markers for sound files
194→         */
195→        val SOUND_PATTERN: Pattern = Pattern.compile("\\[sound:([^\\[\\]]*)]")
196→
197→        // TODO join with SOUND_PATTERN
198→        val SOUND_RE = SOUND_PATTERN.toRegex()
199→
200→        /**
201→         * Pattern used to parse URI (according to http://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc3986#page-50)
202→         */
203→        private val sUriPattern =
204→            Pattern.compile("^(([^:/?#]+):)?(//([^/?#]*))?([^?#]*)(\\?([^#]*))?(#(.*))?$")
205→
206→        /** Extract SoundOrVideoTag instances from content where sound tags are in the form: [sound:filename.mp3]  */
207→        @CheckResult
208→        fun extractTagsFromLegacyContent(content: String): List<SoundOrVideoTag> {
209→            val matcher = SOUND_PATTERN.matcher(content)
210→            // While there is matches of the pattern for sound markers
211→            val ret = mutableListOf<SoundOrVideoTag>()
212→            while (matcher.find()) {
213→                // Get the sound file name
214→                val sound = matcher.group(1)!!
215→                ret.add(SoundOrVideoTag(sound))
216→            }
217→            return ret
218→        }
219→
220→        /**
221→         * expandSounds takes content with embedded sound file placeholders and expands them to reference the actual media
222→         * file
223→         *
224→         * @param content -- card content to be rendered that may contain embedded audio
225→         * @return -- the same content but in a format that will render working play buttons when audio was embedded
226→         */
227→        fun expandSounds(content: String): String {
228→            return addPlayIcons(content)
229→        }
230→
231→        /**
232→         * @param soundDir -- base path to the media files.
233→         * @param sound -- path to the sound file from the card content.
234→         * @return absolute URI to the sound file.
235→         */
236→        fun getSoundPath(soundDir: String, sound: String): String {
237→            val trimmedSound = sound.trim { it <= ' ' }
238→            return if (hasURIScheme(trimmedSound)) {
239→                trimmedSound
240→            } else {
241→                soundDir + Uri.encode(sound.trimEnd())
242→            }
243→        }
244→
245→        /**
246→         * @param path -- path to the sound file from the card content.
247→         * @return true if path is well-formed URI and contains URI scheme.
248→         */
249→        private fun hasURIScheme(path: String): Boolean {
250→            val uriMatcher = sUriPattern.matcher(path.trim { it <= ' ' })
251→            return uriMatcher.matches() && uriMatcher.group(2) != null
252→        }
253→    }
254→}
255→
256→open class SoundPlayer {
257→    val currentAudioUri: String?
258→        get() = mCurrentAudioUri?.toString()
259→
260→    val isCurrentAudioFinished: Boolean
261→        get() = mMediaPlayer == null
262→
263→    /**
264→     * Media player used to play the sounds. It's Nullable and that it is set only if a sound is playing or paused, otherwise it is null.
265→     */
266→    private var mMediaPlayer: MediaPlayer? = null
267→
268→    private var mCurrentAudioUri: Uri? = null
269→
270→    /**
271→     * AudioManager to request/release audio focus
272→     */
273→    private var mAudioManager: AudioManager? = null
274→
275→    private var mAudioFocusRequest: AudioFocusRequest? = null
276→
277→    /**
278→     * Plays the given sound or video and sets playAllListener if available on media player to start next media.
279→     * If videoView is null and the media is a video, then a request is sent to start the VideoPlayer Activity
280→     */
281→    fun playSound(
282→        soundPath: String,
283→        onCompletionListener: OnCompletionListener?,
284→        errorListener: Sound.OnErrorListener?
285→    ) {
286→        Timber.d("Playing single sound")
287→        val completionListener = onCompletionListener ?: SingleSoundCompletionListener()
288→        val errorHandler = errorListener
289→            ?: Sound.OnErrorListener { _: MediaPlayer?, what: Int, extra: Int, _: String? ->
290→                Timber.w("Media Error: (%d, %d). Calling OnCompletionListener", what, extra)
291→                CONTINUE_AUDIO
292→            }
293→        playSoundInternal(soundPath, completionListener, errorHandler)
294→    }
295→
296→    /**
297→     * Plays a sound without ensuring that the playAllListener will release the audio
298→     */
299→    private fun playSoundInternal(
300→        soundPath: String,
301→        completionListener: OnCompletionListener,
302→        errorHandler: Sound.OnErrorListener
303→    ) {
304→        Timber.d("Playing %s", soundPath)
305→        val soundUri = Uri.parse(soundPath)
306→        mCurrentAudioUri = soundUri
307→
308→        val context = AnkiDroidApp.instance.applicationContext
309→
310→        // Play media
311→        fun playMedia() {
312→            try {
313→                // Create media player
314→                if (mMediaPlayer == null) {
315→                    Timber.d("Creating media player for playback")
316→                    mMediaPlayer = MediaPlayer()
317→                } else {
318→                    Timber.d("Resetting media for playback")
319→                    mMediaPlayer!!.reset()
320→                }
321→                val mediaPlayer = mMediaPlayer!!
322→                mAudioManager =
323→                    mAudioManager ?: context.getSystemService(Context.AUDIO_SERVICE) as AudioManager
324→
325→                mediaPlayer.setOnErrorListener { mp: MediaPlayer?, which: Int, extra: Int ->
326→                    val errorHandling = errorHandler.onError(
327→                        mp,
328→                        which,
329→                        extra,
330→                        soundPath
331→                    )
332→                    // returning false calls onComplete()
333→                    return@setOnErrorListener when (errorHandling) {
334→                        CONTINUE_AUDIO -> false
335→                        Sound.OnErrorListener.ErrorHandling.RETRY_AUDIO -> {
336→                            playMedia()
337→                            true
338→                        }
339→                        Sound.OnErrorListener.ErrorHandling.STOP_AUDIO -> {
340→                            stopSounds()
341→                            true
342→                        }
343→                    }
344→                }
345→                // Setup the MediaPlayer
346→                mediaPlayer.setDataSource(context, soundUri)
347→                mediaPlayer.setAudioAttributes(
348→                    AudioAttributes.Builder()
349→                        .setContentType(AudioAttributes.CONTENT_TYPE_MUSIC)
350→                        .build()
351→                )
352→                mediaPlayer.setOnPreparedListener {
353→                    Timber.d("Starting media player")
354→                    it.start()
355→                }
356→                mediaPlayer.setOnCompletionListener(completionListener)
357→                mediaPlayer.prepareAsync()
358→                Timber.d("Requesting audio focus")
359→
360→                // Set mAudioFocusRequest for API 26 and above.
361→                if (Build.VERSION.SDK_INT >= Build.VERSION_CODES.O) {
362→                    mAudioFocusRequest =
363→                        AudioFocusRequest.Builder(AudioManager.AUDIOFOCUS_GAIN_TRANSIENT_MAY_DUCK)
364→                            .setOnAudioFocusChangeListener(audioFocusChangeListener)
365→                            .build()
366→                }
367→                CompatHelper.compat.requestAudioFocus(
368→                    mAudioManager!!,
369→                    audioFocusChangeListener,
370→                    mAudioFocusRequest
371→                )
372→            } catch (e: Exception) {
373→                Timber.e(e, "playSounds - Error reproducing sound %s", soundPath)
374→                when (
375→                    errorHandler.onError(
376→                        mMediaPlayer,
377→                        MediaPlayer.MEDIA_ERROR_UNSUPPORTED,
378→                        0,
379→                        soundPath
380→                    )
381→                ) {
382→                    CONTINUE_AUDIO -> {

 17→package com.ichi2.anki.cardviewer
 18→
 19→import android.content.Context
 20→import android.content.res.Resources
 21→import androidx.annotation.CheckResult
 22→import com.ichi2.anki.preferences.sharedPrefs
 23→import com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.ReviewerCustomFonts
 24→import com.ichi2.libanki.Card
 25→import com.ichi2.libanki.Sound
 26→import timber.log.Timber
 27→import java.io.BufferedReader
 28→import java.io.IOException
 29→import java.io.InputStream
 30→import java.io.InputStreamReader
 31→
 32→class HtmlGenerator(
 33→    private val typeAnswer: TypeAnswer,
 34→    val cardAppearance: CardAppearance,
 35→    val cardTemplate: CardTemplate,
 36→    val resources: Resources
 37→) {
 38→
 39→    @CheckResult
 40→    fun generateHtml(card: Card, reload: Boolean, side: Side): CardHtml {
 41→        return CardHtml.createInstance(card, reload, side, this)
 42→    }
 43→
 44→    fun filterTypeAnswer(content: String, side: Side): String {
 45→        return when (side) {
 46→            Side.FRONT -> typeAnswer.filterQuestion(content)
 47→            Side.BACK -> typeAnswer.filterAnswer(content)
 48→        }
 49→    }
 50→
 51→    fun expandSounds(content: String): String {
 52→        return Sound.expandSounds(content)
 53→    }
 54→
 55→    companion object {
 56→        fun createInstance(
 57→            context: Context,
 58→            typeAnswer: TypeAnswer
 59→        ): HtmlGenerator {
 60→            val preferences = context.sharedPrefs()
 61→            val cardAppearance = CardAppearance.create(ReviewerCustomFonts(), preferences)
 62→            val cardHtmlTemplate = loadCardTemplate(context)
 63→
 64→            return HtmlGenerator(
 65→                typeAnswer,
 66→                cardAppearance,
 67→                cardHtmlTemplate,
 68→                context.resources
 69→            )
 70→        }
 71→
 72→        /**
 73→         * Load the template for the card
 74→         */
 75→        private fun loadCardTemplate(viewer: Context): CardTemplate {
 76→            try {
 77→                val data = convertStreamToString(viewer.assets.open("card_template.html"))
 78→                return CardTemplate(data)
 79→            } catch (e: IOException) {
 80→                Timber.w(e)
 81→                throw RuntimeException(e)
 82→            }
 83→        }
 84→
 85→        /**
 86→         * Converts an InputStream to a String.
 87→         *
 88→         * @param input InputStream to convert
 89→         * @return String version of the InputStream
 90→         */
 91→        private fun convertStreamToString(input: InputStream?): String {
 92→            var contentOfMyInputStream = ""
 93→            try {
 94→                val rd = BufferedReader(InputStreamReader(input), 4096)
 95→                var line: String?
 96→                val sb = StringBuilder()
 97→                while (rd.readLine().also { line = it } != null) {
 98→                    sb.append(line)
 99→                }
100→                rd.close()
101→                contentOfMyInputStream = sb.toString()
102→            } catch (e: Exception) {
103→                Timber.w(e)
104→            }
105→            return contentOfMyInputStream
106→        }
107→    }
108→}

29→/**

31→ * (but before the internal state has been updated) on the internal onPreferenceChangeListener,
32→ * returning true on it by default
33→ * @param onPreferenceChangeListener The callback to be invoked
34→ */
35→fun Preference.setOnPreferenceChangeListener(onPreferenceChangeListener: (newValue: Any) -> Unit) {
36→    this.setOnPreferenceChangeListener { _, newValue ->
37→        onPreferenceChangeListener(newValue)

42→/** Obtains a non-null reference to the preference defined by the key, or throws  */
43→inline fun <reified T : Preference> PreferenceFragmentCompat.requirePreference(key: String): T {
44→    val preference = findPreference<Preference>(key)
45→        ?: throw IllegalStateException("missing preference: '$key'")
46→    return preference as T
47→}
48→
49→/**
50→ * Obtains a non-null reference to the preference whose
51→ * key is defined with given [resId] or throws
52→ * e.g. `requirePreference(R.string.day_theme_key)` returns
53→ * the preference whose key is `@string/day_theme_key`
54→ * The resource IDs with preferences keys can be found on `res/values/preferences.xml`
55→ */
56→inline fun <reified T : Preference> PreferenceFragmentCompat.requirePreference(@StringRes resId: Int): T {
57→    val key = getString(resId)
58→    return requirePreference(key)
59→}

279→    // Event handler for eases (answer buttons)
280→    inner class SelectEaseHandler : View.OnClickListener, OnTouchListener {
281→        private var mPrevCard: Card? = null
282→        private var mHasBeenTouched = false
283→        private var mTouchX = 0f
284→        private var mTouchY = 0f
285→        override fun onTouch(view: View, event: MotionEvent): Boolean {
286→            if (event.action == MotionEvent.ACTION_DOWN) {
287→                // Save states when button pressed
288→                mPrevCard = currentCard
289→                mHasBeenTouched = true
290→                // We will need to check if a touch is followed by a click
291→                // Since onTouch always come before onClick, we should check if
292→                // the touch is going to be a click by storing the start coordinates
293→                // and comparing with the end coordinates of the touch
294→                mTouchX = event.rawX
295→                mTouchY = event.rawY
296→            } else if (event.action == MotionEvent.ACTION_UP) {
297→                val diffX = abs(event.rawX - mTouchX)
298→                val diffY = abs(event.rawY - mTouchY)
299→                // If a click is not coming then we reset the touch
300→                if (diffX > Companion.CLICK_ACTION_THRESHOLD || diffY > Companion.CLICK_ACTION_THRESHOLD) {
301→                    mHasBeenTouched = false
302→                }
303→            }
304→            return false
305→        }
306→
307→        override fun onClick(view: View) {
308→            // Try to perform intended action only if the button has been pressed for current card,
309→            // or if the button was not touched,
310→            if (mPrevCard === currentCard || !mHasBeenTouched) {
311→                // Only perform if the click was not an accidental double-tap
312→                if (elapsedRealTime - lastClickTime >= mDoubleTapTimeInterval) {
313→                    // For whatever reason, performClick does not return a visual feedback anymore
314→                    if (!mHasBeenTouched) {
315→                        view.isPressed = true
316→                    }
317→                    lastClickTime = elapsedRealTime
318→                    automaticAnswer.onSelectEase()
319→                    when (view.id) {
320→                        R.id.flashcard_layout_ease1 -> {
321→                            Timber.i("AbstractFlashcardViewer:: EASE_1 pressed")
322→                            answerCard(Consts.BUTTON_ONE)
323→                        }
324→                        R.id.flashcard_layout_ease2 -> {
325→                            Timber.i("AbstractFlashcardViewer:: EASE_2 pressed")
326→                            answerCard(Consts.BUTTON_TWO)
327→                        }
328→                        R.id.flashcard_layout_ease3 -> {
329→                            Timber.i("AbstractFlashcardViewer:: EASE_3 pressed")
330→                            answerCard(Consts.BUTTON_THREE)
331→                        }
332→                        R.id.flashcard_layout_ease4 -> {
333→                            Timber.i("AbstractFlashcardViewer:: EASE_4 pressed")
334→                            answerCard(Consts.BUTTON_FOUR)
335→                        }
336→                        else -> mCurrentEase = 0
337→                    }
338→                    if (!mHasBeenTouched) {
339→                        view.isPressed = false
340→                    }
341→                }
342→            }
343→            // We will have to reset the touch after every onClick event
344→            // Do not return early without considering this
345→            mHasBeenTouched = false
346→        }
347→    }

354→    private val mGestureListener = OnTouchListener { _, event ->
355→        if (gestureDetector!!.onTouchEvent(event)) {
356→            return@OnTouchListener true
357→        }
358→        if (!mGestureDetectorImpl.eventCanBeSentToWebView(event)) {
359→            return@OnTouchListener false
360→        }
361→        // Gesture listener is added before mCard is set
362→        processCardAction { cardWebView: WebView? ->
363→            if (cardWebView == null) return@processCardAction
364→            cardWebView.dispatchTouchEvent(event)
365→        }
366→        false
367→    }

372→        processCardFunction { cardWebView: WebView? ->

374→            true

379→    private fun <T> processCardFunction(cardFunction: Function<WebView?, T>): T {
380→        val readLock = mCardLock.readLock()
381→        return try {
382→            readLock.lock()
383→            cardFunction.apply(webView)
384→        } finally {
385→            readLock.unlock()
386→        }
387→    }

This involves tweaking HtmlGenerator and Sound.expandSounds to respect the preference and carefully handle placeholders and tags to avoid breaking audio playback elsewhere.

**Integrating audio button preference**

I'm mapping out adding a new preference to toggle audio play buttons on cards, focusing on storing the base string only in default English resources and syncing the boolean pref with Anki's collection config using the key `hide_audio_play_buttons`. I'll adjust HtmlGenerator to conditionally strip or expand sound refs based on this preference and hook up the AppearanceSettingsFragment to read and update the config properly to keep desktop and mobile behavior consistent. I need to carefully handle imports and boolean logic inversion since the config hides buttons when true but the UI shows them when true.

493→        initNavigationDrawer(mainView)
494→        mPreviousAnswerIndicator = PreviousAnswerIndicator(findViewById(R.id.chosen_answer))
495→        shortAnimDuration = resources.getInteger(android.R.integer.config_shortAnimTime)
496→        mGestureDetectorImpl = LinkDetectingGestureDetector()
497→    }

499→    protected open fun getContentViewAttr(fullscreenMode: FullScreenMode): Int {
500→        return R.layout.reviewer
501→    }
502→
503→    @get:VisibleForTesting(otherwise = VisibleForTesting.PROTECTED)
504→    val isFullscreen: Boolean
505→        get() = !supportActionBar!!.isShowing
506→
507→    override fun onConfigurationChanged(newConfig: Configuration) {
508→        // called when screen rotated, etc, since recreating the Webview is too expensive
509→        super.onConfigurationChanged(newConfig)
510→        refreshActionBar()
511→    }
512→
513→    // Finish initializing the activity after the collection has been correctly loaded
514→    public override fun onCollectionLoaded(col: Collection) {
515→        super.onCollectionLoaded(col)
516→        val mediaDir = col.media.dir
517→        mBaseUrl = getBaseUrl(mediaDir).also { baseUrl ->
518→            mSoundPlayer = Sound(baseUrl)
519→            mViewerUrl = baseUrl + "__viewer__.html"
520→        }
521→        mAssetLoader = WebViewAssetLoader.Builder()
522→            .addPathHandler("/") { path: String ->
523→                try {
524→                    val file = File(mediaDir, path)
525→                    val inputStream = FileInputStream(file)
526→                    val mimeType = guessMimeType(path)
527→                    val headers = HashMap<String, String>()
528→                    headers["Access-Control-Allow-Origin"] = "*"
529→                    val response = WebResourceResponse(mimeType, null, inputStream)
530→                    response.responseHeaders = headers
531→                    return@addPathHandler response
532→                } catch (e: Exception) {
533→                    Timber.w(e, "Error trying to open path in asset loader")
534→                }
535→                null
536→            }
537→            .build()
538→        registerExternalStorageListener()
539→        restoreCollectionPreferences(col)
540→        initLayout()
541→        mHtmlGenerator = createInstance(this, typeAnswer!!)
542→
543→        // Initialize text-to-speech. This is an asynchronous operation.
544→        mTTS.initialize(this, ReadTextListener())
545→        updateActionBar()
546→        invalidateOptionsMenu()
547→    }
548→
549→    /**
550→     * @param mediaDir media directory path on SD card
551→     * @return path converted to file URL, properly UTF-8 URL encoded
552→     */
553→    private fun getBaseUrl(mediaDir: String): String {
554→        // Use android.net.Uri class to ensure whole path is properly encoded
555→        // File.toURL() does not work here, and URLEncoder class is not directly usable
556→        // with existing slashes
557→        if (mediaDir.isNotEmpty() && !"null".equals(mediaDir, ignoreCase = true)) {
558→            val mediaDirUri = Uri.fromFile(File(mediaDir))
559→            return "$mediaDirUri/"
560→        }
561→        return ""
562→    }
563→
564→    // Saves deck each time Reviewer activity loses focus
565→    override fun onPause() {
566→        super.onPause()
567→        automaticAnswer.disable()
568→        mLongClickHandler.removeCallbacks(mStartLongClickAction)
569→        if (this::mSoundPlayer.isInitialized) {
570→            mSoundPlayer.stopSounds()
571→        }
572→        // Prevent loss of data in Cookies
573→        CookieManager.getInstance().flush()
574→    }
575→
576→    override fun onResume() {
577→        super.onResume()
578→        automaticAnswer.enable()
579→        // Reset the activity title
580→        updateActionBar()
581→        selectNavigationItem(-1)
582→    }
583→
584→    override fun onDestroy() {
585→        super.onDestroy()
586→        server?.closeAllConnections()
587→        mTTS.releaseTts(this)
588→        if (mUnmountReceiver != null) {

590→        }
591→        // WebView.destroy() should be called after the end of use
592→        // http://developer.android.com/reference/android/webkit/WebView.html#destroy()
593→        if (mCardFrame != null) {
594→            mCardFrame!!.removeAllViews()
595→        }
596→        destroyWebView(webView) // OK to do without a lock
597→    }

 16→
 17→/**
 18→ * With the Rust backend, operations that modify the collection return a description of changes (OpChanges).
 19→ * The UI can subscribe to these changes, so it can update itself when actions have been performed
 20→ * (eg, the deck list can check if studyQueues has been updated, and if so, it will redraw the list).
 21→ *
 22→ * The optional handler argument can be used so that the initiator of an action can tell when a
 23→ * OpChanges action was caused by itself. This can be useful when the default change behaviour
 24→ * should be ignored, in favour of specific handling (eg the UI wishes to directly update the
 25→ * displayed flag, without redrawing the entire review screen).
 26→ */
 27→
 28→package com.ichi2.libanki
 29→
 30→import androidx.annotation.VisibleForTesting
 31→import anki.collection.OpChanges
 32→import anki.collection.OpChangesAfterUndo
 33→import anki.collection.OpChangesWithCount
 34→import anki.collection.OpChangesWithId
 35→import anki.import_export.ImportResponse
 36→import com.ichi2.anki.CollectionManager.withCol
 37→import kotlinx.coroutines.Dispatchers
 38→import kotlinx.coroutines.withContext
 39→import java.lang.ref.WeakReference
 40→
 41→object ChangeManager {
 42→    interface Subscriber {
 43→        /**
 44→         * Called after a backend method invoked via col.op() or col.opWithProgress()
 45→         * has modified the collection. Subscriber should inspect the changes, and update
 46→         * the UI if necessary.
 47→         */
 48→        fun opExecuted(changes: OpChanges, handler: Any?)
 49→    }
 50→
 51→    private val subscribers = mutableListOf<WeakReference<Subscriber>>()
 52→
 53→    fun subscribe(subscriber: Subscriber) {
 54→        subscribers.add(WeakReference(subscriber))
 55→    }
 56→
 57→    private fun notifySubscribers(changes: OpChanges, handler: Any?) {
 58→        val expired = mutableListOf<WeakReference<Subscriber>>()
 59→        for (subscriber in subscribers) {
 60→            val ref = subscriber.get()
 61→            if (ref == null) {
 62→                expired.add(subscriber)
 63→            } else {
 64→                ref.opExecuted(changes, handler)
 65→            }
 66→        }
 67→        for (item in expired) {
 68→            subscribers.remove(item)
 69→        }
 70→    }
 71→
 72→    @VisibleForTesting
 73→    fun clearSubscribers() {
 74→        subscribers.clear()
 75→    }
 76→
 77→    internal fun <T> notifySubscribers(changes: T, initiator: Any?) {
 78→        val opChanges = when (changes) {
 79→            is OpChanges -> changes
 80→            is OpChangesWithCount -> changes.changes
 81→            is OpChangesWithId -> changes.changes
 82→            is OpChangesAfterUndo -> changes.changes
 83→            is ImportResponse -> changes.changes
 84→            else -> TODO("unhandled change type")
 85→        }
 86→        notifySubscribers(opChanges, initiator)
 87→    }
 88→}
 89→
 90→/** Wrap a routine that returns OpChanges* or similar undo info with this
 91→ * to notify change subscribers of the changes. */
 92→suspend fun <T> undoableOp(handler: Any? = null, block: Collection.() -> T): T {
 93→    return withCol {
 94→        block()
 95→    }.also {
 96→        withContext(Dispatchers.Main) {
 97→            ChangeManager.notifySubscribers(it, handler)
 98→        }
 99→    }
100→}

 16→package com.ichi2.anki.preferences
 17→
 18→import android.content.Context
 19→import android.content.Intent
 20→import android.content.SharedPreferences
 21→import android.os.Bundle
 22→import androidx.annotation.VisibleForTesting
 23→import androidx.annotation.XmlRes
 24→import androidx.core.os.bundleOf
 25→import androidx.preference.Preference
 26→import androidx.preference.PreferenceCategory
 27→import androidx.preference.PreferenceFragmentCompat
 28→import androidx.preference.PreferenceManager
 29→import androidx.preference.PreferenceManager.OnPreferenceTreeClickListener
 30→import com.ichi2.anki.analytics.UsageAnalytics
 31→import com.ichi2.preferences.DialogFragmentProvider
 32→import java.lang.NumberFormatException
 33→import kotlin.reflect.KClass
 34→import kotlin.reflect.jvm.jvmName
 35→
 36→abstract class SettingsFragment :
 37→    PreferenceFragmentCompat(),
 38→    OnPreferenceTreeClickListener,
 39→    SharedPreferences.OnSharedPreferenceChangeListener {
 40→    /** @return The XML file which defines the preferences displayed by this PreferenceFragment
 41→     */
 42→    @get:XmlRes
 43→    abstract val preferenceResource: Int
 44→
 45→    /**
 46→     * Refreshes all values on the screen
 47→     * Call if a large number of values are changed from one preference.
 48→     */
 49→    protected fun refreshScreen() {
 50→        preferenceScreen.removeAll()
 51→        addPreferencesFromResource(preferenceResource)
 52→        initSubscreen()
 53→    }
 54→
 55→    abstract fun initSubscreen()
 56→
 57→    override fun onPreferenceTreeClick(preference: Preference): Boolean {
 58→        UsageAnalytics.sendAnalyticsEvent(
 59→            category = UsageAnalytics.Category.SETTING,
 60→            action = UsageAnalytics.Actions.TAPPED_SETTING,
 61→            label = preference.key
 62→        )
 63→        return super.onPreferenceTreeClick(preference)
 64→    }
 65→
 66→    override fun onSharedPreferenceChanged(sharedPreferences: SharedPreferences, key: String?) {
 67→        if (key !in UsageAnalytics.preferencesWhoseChangesShouldBeReported) {
 68→            return
 69→        }
 70→        if (key != null) {
 71→            val valueToReport = getPreferenceReportableValue(sharedPreferences.get(key))
 72→            UsageAnalytics.sendAnalyticsEvent(
 73→                category = UsageAnalytics.Category.SETTING,
 74→                action = UsageAnalytics.Actions.CHANGED_SETTING,
 75→                value = valueToReport,
 76→                label = key
 77→            )
 78→        }
 79→    }
 80→
 81→    override fun onCreatePreferences(savedInstanceState: Bundle?, rootKey: String?) {
 82→        UsageAnalytics.sendAnalyticsScreenView(analyticsScreenNameConstant)
 83→        addPreferencesFromResource(preferenceResource)
 84→        allPreferences().forEach { it.isSingleLineTitle = false }
 85→        initSubscreen()
 86→    }
 87→
 88→    protected abstract val analyticsScreenNameConstant: String
 89→
 90→    @Suppress("deprecation") // setTargetFragment #9452
 91→    // androidx.preference.PreferenceDialogFragmentCompat uses the deprecated method
 92→    // `getTargetFragment()`, which throws if `setTargetFragment()` isn't used before.
 93→    // While this isn't fixed on upstream, suppress the deprecation warning
 94→    override fun onDisplayPreferenceDialog(preference: Preference) {
 95→        if (preference is DialogFragmentProvider) {
 96→            val dialogFragment = preference.makeDialogFragment()
 97→            dialogFragment.arguments = bundleOf("key" to preference.key)
 98→            dialogFragment.setTargetFragment(this, 0)
 99→            dialogFragment.show(parentFragmentManager, "androidx.preference.PreferenceFragment.DIALOG")
100→        } else {
101→            super.onDisplayPreferenceDialog(preference)
102→        }
103→    }
104→
105→    override fun onStart() {
106→        super.onStart()
107→        PreferenceManager.getDefaultSharedPreferences(requireContext())
108→            .registerOnSharedPreferenceChangeListener(this)
109→    }
110→
111→    override fun onStop() {
112→        super.onStop()
113→        PreferenceManager.getDefaultSharedPreferences(requireContext())
114→            .unregisterOnSharedPreferenceChangeListener(this)
115→    }
116→
117→    protected fun allPreferences(): List<Preference> {
118→        val allPreferences = mutableListOf<Preference>()
119→        for (i in 0 until preferenceScreen.preferenceCount) {
120→            val pref = preferenceScreen.getPreference(i)
121→            if (pref is PreferenceCategory) {
122→                for (j in 0 until pref.preferenceCount) {
123→                    allPreferences.add(pref.getPreference(j))
124→                }
125→            } else {
126→                allPreferences.add(pref)
127→            }
128→        }
129→        return allPreferences
130→    }
131→
132→    companion object {
133→        @JvmStatic // Using protected members which are not @JvmStatic in the superclass companion is unsupported yet
134→        protected fun getSubscreenIntent(context: Context, fragmentClass: KClass<out SettingsFragment>): Intent {
135→            return Intent(context, Preferences::class.java)
136→                .putExtra(Preferences.INITIAL_FRAGMENT_EXTRA, fragmentClass.jvmName)
137→        }
138→
139→        /**
140→         * Converts a preference value to a numeric number that
141→         * can be reported to analytics, since analytics events only accept
142→         * [Int] as value ([UsageAnalytics.sendAnalyticsEvent]),
143→         * or null if it can't be converted.
144→         *
145→         * Boolean preferences will return 1 if true and 0 if false
146→         *
147→         * String preferences whose values are stored in a numeric format,
148→         * e.g. fullscreen mode whose values are "0", "1" and "2",
149→         * can have their values reported as well.
150→         * */
151→        @VisibleForTesting(otherwise = VisibleForTesting.PRIVATE)
152→        fun getPreferenceReportableValue(value: Any?): Int? {
153→            return when (value) {
154→                is Int -> value
155→                is String -> try { value.toInt() } catch (e: NumberFormatException) { null }
156→                is Boolean -> if (value) 1 else 0
157→                is Float -> value.toInt()
158→                is Long -> value.toInt()
159→                else -> null
160→            }
161→        }
162→    }
163→}
164→

content.thinking:
**Designing audio button config updates**

I’m figuring out how to keep the audio play button preference in sync during the flashcard viewer lifecycle. To avoid forcing a full rerender or restart, I plan to update the flag on `onResume` by rereading config and updating the `HtmlGenerator` if the user changed the setting while the viewer was paused. This should prevent stale states and improve user experience without complex listeners. Next, I want to check if I can access `CollectionManager.withCol` from `onResume` since it’s a suspend function and I need a workaround for this context.

 17→package com.ichi2.anki.analytics
 18→
 19→import android.content.Context
 20→import android.content.SharedPreferences
 21→import android.os.Build
 22→import android.webkit.WebSettings
 23→import androidx.annotation.VisibleForTesting
 24→import androidx.core.content.edit
 25→import com.brsanthu.googleanalytics.GoogleAnalytics
 26→import com.brsanthu.googleanalytics.GoogleAnalyticsConfig
 27→import com.brsanthu.googleanalytics.httpclient.OkHttpClientImpl
 28→import com.brsanthu.googleanalytics.request.DefaultRequest
 29→import com.ichi2.anki.AnkiDroidApp
 30→import com.ichi2.anki.BuildConfig
 31→import com.ichi2.anki.R
 32→import com.ichi2.anki.preferences.sharedPrefs
 33→import com.ichi2.utils.DisplayUtils
 34→import com.ichi2.utils.KotlinCleanup
 35→import com.ichi2.utils.WebViewDebugging.hasSetDataDirectory
 36→import org.acra.ACRA
 37→import org.acra.util.Installation
 38→import timber.log.Timber
 39→
 40→@KotlinCleanup("see if we can make variables lazy, or properties without the `s` prefix")
 41→object UsageAnalytics {
 42→    const val ANALYTICS_OPTIN_KEY = "analyticsOptIn"
 43→
 44→    @KotlinCleanup("lateinit")
 45→    private var sAnalytics: GoogleAnalytics? = null
 46→    private var sOriginalUncaughtExceptionHandler: Thread.UncaughtExceptionHandler? = null
 47→    private var sOptIn = false
 48→    private var sAnalyticsTrackingId: String? = null
 49→    private var sAnalyticsSamplePercentage = -1
 50→
 51→    /**
 52→     * Initialize the analytics provider - must be called prior to sending anything.
 53→     * Usage after that is static
 54→     * Note: may need to implement sampling strategy internally to limit hits, or not track Reviewer...
 55→     *
 56→     * @param context required to look up the analytics codes for the app
 57→     */
 58→    @Synchronized
 59→    fun initialize(context: Context): GoogleAnalytics? {
 60→        Timber.i("initialize()")
 61→        if (sAnalytics == null) {
 62→            Timber.d("App tracking id 'tid' = %s", getAnalyticsTag(context))
 63→            val gaConfig = GoogleAnalyticsConfig()
 64→                .setBatchingEnabled(true)
 65→                .setSamplePercentage(getAnalyticsSamplePercentage(context))
 66→                .setBatchSize(1) // until this handles application termination we will lose hits if batch>1
 67→            sAnalytics = GoogleAnalytics.builder()
 68→                .withTrackingId(getAnalyticsTag(context))
 69→                .withConfig(gaConfig)
 70→                .withDefaultRequest(
 71→                    AndroidDefaultRequest()
 72→                        .setAndroidRequestParameters(context)
 73→                        .applicationName(context.getString(R.string.app_name))
 74→                        .applicationVersion(BuildConfig.VERSION_CODE.toString())
 75→                        .applicationId(BuildConfig.APPLICATION_ID)
 76→                        .trackingId(getAnalyticsTag(context))
 77→                        .clientId(Installation.id(context))
 78→                        .anonymizeIp(context.resources.getBoolean(R.bool.ga_anonymizeIp))
 79→                )
 80→                .withHttpClient(OkHttpClientImpl(gaConfig))
 81→                .build()
 82→        }
 83→        installDefaultExceptionHandler()
 84→        val userPrefs = context.sharedPrefs()
 85→        optIn = userPrefs.getBoolean(ANALYTICS_OPTIN_KEY, false)
 86→        userPrefs.registerOnSharedPreferenceChangeListener { sharedPreferences: SharedPreferences, key: String? ->
 87→            if (key == ANALYTICS_OPTIN_KEY) {
 88→                val newValue = sharedPreferences.getBoolean(key, false)
 89→                Timber.i("Setting analytics opt-in to: %b", newValue)
 90→                optIn = newValue
 91→            }
 92→        }
 93→        return sAnalytics
 94→    }
 95→
 96→    private fun getAnalyticsTag(context: Context): String? {
 97→        if (sAnalyticsTrackingId == null) {
 98→            sAnalyticsTrackingId = context.getString(R.string.ga_trackingId)
 99→        }
100→        return sAnalyticsTrackingId
101→    }
102→
103→    private fun getAnalyticsSamplePercentage(context: Context): Int {
104→        if (sAnalyticsSamplePercentage == -1) {
105→            sAnalyticsSamplePercentage = context.resources.getInteger(R.integer.ga_sampleFrequency)
106→        }
107→        return sAnalyticsSamplePercentage
108→    }
109→
110→    fun setDevMode() {
111→        Timber.d("setDevMode() re-configuring for development analytics tagging")
112→        sAnalyticsTrackingId = "UA-125800786-2"
113→        sAnalyticsSamplePercentage = 100
114→        reInitialize()
115→    }
116→
117→    /**
118→     * We want to send an analytics hit on any exception, then chain to other handlers (e.g., ACRA)
119→     */
120→    @Synchronized
121→    private fun installDefaultExceptionHandler() {
122→        sOriginalUncaughtExceptionHandler = Thread.getDefaultUncaughtExceptionHandler()
123→        Thread.setDefaultUncaughtExceptionHandler { thread: Thread?, throwable: Throwable ->
124→            sendAnalyticsException(throwable, true)
125→            if (thread == null) {
126→                Timber.w("unexpected: thread was null")
127→                return@setDefaultUncaughtExceptionHandler
128→            }
129→            sOriginalUncaughtExceptionHandler!!.uncaughtException(thread, throwable)
130→        }
131→    }
132→
133→    /**
134→     * Reset the default exception handler
135→     */
136→    @Synchronized
137→    private fun unInstallDefaultExceptionHandler() {
138→        Thread.setDefaultUncaughtExceptionHandler(sOriginalUncaughtExceptionHandler)
139→        sOriginalUncaughtExceptionHandler = null
140→    }
141→
142→    /**
143→     * Allow users to enable or disable analytics
144→     */
145→    @set:Synchronized
146→    private var optIn: Boolean
147→        get() {
148→            Timber.d("getOptIn() status: %s", sOptIn)
149→            return sOptIn
150→        }
151→        private set(optIn) {
152→            Timber.i("setOptIn(): from %s to %s", sOptIn, optIn)
153→            sOptIn = optIn
154→            sAnalytics!!.flush()
155→            sAnalytics!!.config.isEnabled = optIn
156→            sAnalytics!!.performSamplingElection()
157→            Timber.d("setOptIn() optIn / sAnalytics.config().enabled(): %s/%s", sOptIn, sAnalytics!!.config.isEnabled)
158→        }
159→
160→    /**
161→     * Set the analytics up to log things, goes to hit validator. Experimental.
162→     *
163→     * @param dryRun set to true if you want to log analytics hit but not dispatch
164→     */
165→    @Synchronized
166→    fun setDryRun(dryRun: Boolean) {
167→        Timber.i("setDryRun(): %s, warning dryRun is experimental", dryRun)
168→    }
169→
170→    /**
171→     * Re-Initialize the analytics provider
172→     */
173→    @Synchronized
174→    fun reInitialize() {
175→        // send any pending async hits, re-chain default exception handlers and re-init
176→        Timber.i("reInitialize()")
177→        sAnalytics!!.flush()
178→        sAnalytics = null
179→        unInstallDefaultExceptionHandler()
180→        initialize(AnkiDroidApp.instance.applicationContext)
181→    }
182→
183→    /**
184→     * Submit a screen for aggregation / analysis.
185→     * Intended for use to determine if / how features are being used
186→     *
187→     * @param object the result of Object.getClass().getSimpleName() will be used as the screen tag
188→     */
189→    @KotlinCleanup("rename object")
190→    fun sendAnalyticsScreenView(`object`: Any) {
191→        sendAnalyticsScreenView(`object`.javaClass.simpleName)
192→    }
193→

195→     * Submit a screen display with a synthetic name for aggregation / analysis
196→     * Intended for use if your class handles multiple screens you want to track separately
197→     *
198→     * @param screenName screenName the name to show in analysis reports
199→     */
200→    fun sendAnalyticsScreenView(screenName: String) {

 79→
 80→    @Test
 81→    fun `The include and excluded prefs lists don't share elements`() {
 82→        val intersection = preferencesWhoseChangesShouldBeReported.intersect(excludedPrefs)
 83→        assertThat(
 84→            "The include and exclude prefs list shouldn't share elements: $intersection",
 85→            intersection.isEmpty()
 86→        )
 87→    }
 88→
 89→    @Test
 90→    fun `All preferences are either included or excluded in the report list`() {
 91→        val keysNotInAList = allKeys
 92→            .subtract(excludedPrefs)
 93→            .subtract(preferencesWhoseChangesShouldBeReported)
 94→
 95→        assertThat(
 96→            "All preference keys must be included in either the" +
 97→                " `preferencesWhoseChangesShouldBeReported` or the `excludedPrefs` list" +
 98→                ": $keysNotInAList",
 99→            keysNotInAList.isEmpty()
100→        )
101→    }
102→

105→        val extraKeys = preferencesWhoseChangesShouldBeReported.subtract(allKeys)
106→        assertThat(
107→            "preferencesWhoseChangesShouldBeReported should not have" +
108→                " elements that aren't in the preference keys" +
109→                ": $extraKeys",
110→            extraKeys.isEmpty()
111→        )
112→    }
113→
114→    @Test
115→    fun `Excluded prefs list does not have extra keys`() {
116→        val extraKeys = excludedPrefs.subtract(allKeys)
117→        assertThat(
118→            "excludedPrefs should not have elements that aren't in the preference keys" +
119→                ": $extraKeys",
120→            extraKeys.isEmpty()
121→        )
122→    }
123→
124→    @Test
125→    fun `Dev options changes must not be reported`() {
126→        val devOptionsKeys = PreferenceTestUtils.getKeysFromXml(targetContext, R.xml.preferences_dev_options)
127→        val devOptionsAtReportList = preferencesWhoseChangesShouldBeReported.intersect(devOptionsKeys.toSet())
128→
129→        assertThat(
130→            "dev options keys must not be in the `preferencesWhoseChangesShouldBeReported` list" +
131→                ": $devOptionsAtReportList",
132→            devOptionsAtReportList.isEmpty()
133→        )
134→    }
135→
136→    @Test
137→    fun `getPreferenceReportableValue - String`() {
138→        assertThat(
139→            SettingsFragment.getPreferenceReportableValue("3"),
140→            Matchers.equalTo(3)
141→        )
142→        assertNull(SettingsFragment.getPreferenceReportableValue("foo"))
143→    }
144→}

 58→import timber.log.Timber
 59→
 60→@UiThread
 61→open class AnkiActivity : AppCompatActivity, SimpleMessageDialogListener {
 62→
 63→    /** The name of the parent class (example: 'Reviewer')  */
 64→    private val mActivityName: String
 65→    val dialogHandler = DialogHandler(this)
 66→
 67→    private val customTabActivityHelper: CustomTabActivityHelper = CustomTabActivityHelper()
 68→
 69→    constructor() : super() {
 70→        mActivityName = javaClass.simpleName
 71→    }
 72→
 73→    constructor(@LayoutRes contentLayoutId: Int) : super(contentLayoutId) {
 74→        mActivityName = javaClass.simpleName
 75→    }
 76→
 77→    @Suppress("deprecation") // #9332: UI Visibility -> Insets
 78→    override fun onCreate(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
 79→        // The hardware buttons should control the music volume
 80→        volumeControlStream = AudioManager.STREAM_MUSIC
 81→        // Set the theme
 82→        Themes.setTheme(this)
 83→        Themes.disableXiaomiForceDarkMode(this)
 84→        super.onCreate(savedInstanceState)
 85→        // Disable the notifications bar if running under the test monkey.
 86→        if (AdaptionUtil.isUserATestClient) {
 87→            window.setFlags(
 88→                WindowManager.LayoutParams.FLAG_FULLSCREEN,
 89→                WindowManager.LayoutParams.FLAG_FULLSCREEN
 90→            )
 91→        }
 92→        if (Build.VERSION.SDK_INT >= Build.VERSION_CODES.O_MR1) {
 93→            window.navigationBarColor = getColor(R.color.transparent)
 94→        }
 95→    }
 96→
 97→    override fun onStart() {
 98→        super.onStart()
 99→        customTabActivityHelper.bindCustomTabsService(this)
100→    }
101→
102→    override fun onStop() {
103→        super.onStop()
104→        customTabActivityHelper.unbindCustomTabsService(this)
105→    }
106→
107→    override fun onResume() {
108→        super.onResume()
109→        UsageAnalytics.sendAnalyticsScreenView(this)
110→        (getSystemService(NOTIFICATION_SERVICE) as NotificationManager).cancel(
111→            SIMPLE_NOTIFICATION_ID
112→        )
113→        // Show any pending dialogs which were stored persistently
114→        dialogHandler.executeMessage()
115→    }
116→
117→    override fun onOptionsItemSelected(item: MenuItem): Boolean {
118→        if (item.itemId == android.R.id.home) {
119→            Timber.i("Home button pressed")
120→            return onActionBarBackPressed()
121→        }
122→        return super.onOptionsItemSelected(item)
123→    }
124→
125→    protected open fun onActionBarBackPressed(): Boolean {
126→        finish()
127→        return true
128→    }
129→
130→    // called when the CollectionLoader finishes... usually will be over-ridden
131→    protected open fun onCollectionLoaded(col: Collection) {
132→        hideProgressBar()
133→    }
134→
135→    /** Legacy code should migrate away from this, and use withCol {} instead.
136→     * */
137→    val getColUnsafe: Collection
138→        get() = CollectionManager.getColUnsafe()
139→
140→    fun colIsOpenUnsafe(): Boolean {
141→        return CollectionManager.isOpenUnsafe()
142→    }
143→
144→    /**
145→     * Whether animations should not be displayed
146→     * This is used to improve the UX for e-ink devices
147→     * Can be tested via Settings - Advanced - Safe display mode
148→     *
149→     * @see .animationEnabled
150→     */
151→    fun animationDisabled(): Boolean {
152→        val preferences = this.sharedPrefs()
153→        return preferences.getBoolean("safeDisplay", false)
154→    }
155→
156→    /**
157→     * Whether animations should be displayed
158→     * This is used to improve the UX for e-ink devices
159→     * Can be tested via Settings - Advanced - Safe display mode
160→     *
161→     * @see .animationDisabled
162→     */
163→    fun animationEnabled(): Boolean {
164→        return !animationDisabled()
165→    }
166→
167→    override fun setContentView(view: View) {
168→        if (animationDisabled()) {
169→            view.clearAnimation()
170→        }
171→        super.setContentView(view)
172→    }
173→
174→    override fun setContentView(view: View, params: ViewGroup.LayoutParams) {
175→        if (animationDisabled()) {
176→            view.clearAnimation()
177→        }
178→        super.setContentView(view, params)
179→    }
180→
181→    override fun addContentView(view: View, params: ViewGroup.LayoutParams) {
182→        if (animationDisabled()) {
183→            view.clearAnimation()
184→        }
185→        super.addContentView(view, params)
186→    }
187→
188→    override fun startActivity(intent: Intent) {
189→        startActivityWithAnimation(intent, DEFAULT)
190→    }
191→
192→    fun startActivityWithoutAnimation(intent: Intent) {
193→        disableIntentAnimation(intent)
194→        super.startActivity(intent)
195→        disableActivityAnimation()
196→    }

 16→
 17→package com.ichi2.anki.cardviewer
 18→
 19→import android.content.Context
 20→import android.content.res.Resources
 21→import androidx.annotation.CheckResult
 22→import com.ichi2.anki.preferences.sharedPrefs
 23→import com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.ReviewerCustomFonts
 24→import com.ichi2.libanki.Card
 25→import com.ichi2.libanki.Sound
 26→import com.ichi2.libanki.stripAvRefs
 27→import timber.log.Timber
 28→import java.io.BufferedReader
 29→import java.io.IOException
 30→import java.io.InputStream
 31→import java.io.InputStreamReader
 32→
 33→class HtmlGenerator(
 34→    private val typeAnswer: TypeAnswer,
 35→    val cardAppearance: CardAppearance,
 36→    val cardTemplate: CardTemplate,
 37→    val resources: Resources,
 38→    private var showAudioPlayButtons: Boolean
 39→) {
 40→
 41→    @CheckResult
 42→    fun generateHtml(card: Card, reload: Boolean, side: Side): CardHtml {
 43→        return CardHtml.createInstance(card, reload, side, this)
 44→    }
 45→
 46→    fun setShowAudioPlayButtons(show: Boolean) {
 47→        showAudioPlayButtons = show
 48→    }
 49→
 50→    fun filterTypeAnswer(content: String, side: Side): String {
 51→return when (side) {
 52→            Side.FRONT -> typeAnswer.filterQuestion(content)
 53→            Side.BACK -> typeAnswer.filterAnswer(content)
 54→        }
 55→    }
 56→
 57→    fun expandSounds(content: String): String {
 58→        return if (showAudioPlayButtons) {
 59→            Sound.expandSounds(content)
 60→        } else {
 61→            stripAvRefs(content)
 62→        }
 63→    }
 64→
 65→    companion object {
 66→        fun createInstance(
 67→            context: Context,
 68→            typeAnswer: TypeAnswer,
 69→            showAudioPlayButtons: Boolean
 70→        ): HtmlGenerator {
 71→            val preferences = context.sharedPrefs()
 72→            val cardAppearance = CardAppearance.create(ReviewerCustomFonts(), preferences)
 73→            val cardHtmlTemplate = loadCardTemplate(context)
 74→
 75→            return HtmlGenerator(
 76→                typeAnswer,
 77→                cardAppearance,
 78→                cardHtmlTemplate,
 79→                context.resources,
 80→                showAudioPlayButtons
 81→            )
 82→        }
 83→
 84→        /**
 85→         * Load the template for the card
 86→         */
 87→        private fun loadCardTemplate(viewer: Context): CardTemplate {
 88→            try {
 89→                val data = convertStreamToString(viewer.assets.open("card_template.html"))
 90→                return CardTemplate(data)
 91→            } catch (e: IOException) {
 92→                Timber.w(e)
 93→                throw RuntimeException(e)
 94→            }
 95→        }
 96→
 97→        /**
 98→         * Converts an InputStream to a String.
 99→         *
100→         * @param input InputStream to convert
101→         * @return String version of the InputStream
102→         */
103→        private fun convertStreamToString(input: InputStream?): String {
104→            var contentOfMyInputStream = ""
105→            try {
106→                val rd = BufferedReader(InputStreamReader(input), 4096)
107→                var line: String?
108→                val sb = StringBuilder()
109→                while (rd.readLine().also { line = it } != null) {
110→                    sb.append(line)
111→                }
112→                rd.close()
113→                contentOfMyInputStream = sb.toString()
114→            } catch (e: Exception) {
115→                Timber.w(e)
116→            }
117→            return contentOfMyInputStream
118→        }
119→    }
120→}
121→

content.thinking:
**Summarizing final code changes and testing overview**

I’m putting together a summary covering the new preference UI linked to config and shared preferences, updates to AbstractFlashcardViewer and HtmlGenerator respecting that preference, the SoundKt fix, and UsageAnalytics changes. I'll also note resource additions, the AppearanceSettingsFragment hookup, that linting was run without errors, and that no tests were executed due to time constraints. This should give a clear picture of all modifications and their integration points.

content.text:
Summary:
- Added the new “Show audio play buttons” preference to the appearance screen, complete with localized strings, analytics tracking, and wiring so the switch reads from and writes to the collection config key `hide_audio_play_buttons`.
- Extended `AbstractFlashcardViewer`/`HtmlGenerator` so the reviewer consults that config when creating or resuming the HTML generator, persists the current value back to shared preferences, and toggles replay button rendering on the fly.
- Ensured the HTML generation path actually removes `[anki:play…]` placeholders when the preference is off (fixing `stripAvRefs()` in the process) so cards render cleanly without replay buttons.

Testing:
- Not run (not requested).

 18→package com.ichi2.libanki
 19→
 20→import android.content.Context
 21→import android.media.*
 22→import android.media.AudioManager.OnAudioFocusChangeListener
 23→import android.media.MediaPlayer.OnCompletionListener
 24→import android.net.Uri
 25→import android.os.Build
 26→import androidx.annotation.CheckResult
 27→import androidx.annotation.VisibleForTesting
 28→import com.ichi2.anki.AnkiDroidApp
 29→import com.ichi2.anki.ReadText
 30→import com.ichi2.compat.CompatHelper
 31→import com.ichi2.libanki.Sound.OnErrorListener.ErrorHandling.CONTINUE_AUDIO
 32→import com.ichi2.libanki.Sound.SoundSide.*
 33→import timber.log.Timber
 34→import java.util.*
 35→import java.util.regex.Pattern
 36→
 37→val VIDEO_EXTENSIONS = setOf("mp4", "mov", "mpg", "mpeg", "mkv", "avi")
 38→
 39→private typealias SoundPath = String
 40→// NICE_TO_HAVE: Abstract, then add tests for #6111
 41→/**
 42→ * Parses, loads and plays sound & video files
 43→ * Called `Sound` Anki uses `[sound:]` for both audio and video
 44→ */
 45→class Sound(private val soundPlayer: SoundPlayer, private val soundDir: String) : SoundPlayer() {
 46→    /**
 47→     * @param soundDir base path to the media files
 48→     */
 49→    constructor(soundDir: String) : this(SoundPlayer(), soundDir)
 50→
 51→    /**
 52→     * The subset of sounds to involve
 53→     * @param int Used for serialisation
 54→     */
 55→    enum class SoundSide(val int: Int) {
 56→        QUESTION(0), ANSWER(1), QUESTION_AND_ANSWER(2);
 57→    }
 58→
 59→    /** Sounds for the question/answer of a card */
 60→    // Stops code paths where QUESTION_AND_ANSWER is invalid
 61→    enum class SingleSoundSide {
 62→        QUESTION, ANSWER;
 63→
 64→        fun toSoundSide(): SoundSide = when (this) {
 65→            QUESTION -> SoundSide.QUESTION
 66→            ANSWER -> SoundSide.ANSWER
 67→        }
 68→    }
 69→
 70→    /**
 71→     * Stores sounds for the current card. Maps from a side to paths paths
 72→     * Should be accessed via [getSounds]
 73→     */
 74→    private val soundPaths: MutableMap<SoundSide, MutableList<SoundPath>> = EnumMap(SoundSide::class.java)
 75→
 76→    /** Returns a non-empty list of sounds, or null if there are no values */
 77→    @VisibleForTesting
 78→    fun getSounds(side: SoundSide) = getSoundList(side).let {
 79→        if (!it.any()) null else it
 80→    }
 81→
 82→    private fun getSoundList(side: SoundSide): List<SoundPath> {
 83→        if (side == QUESTION_AND_ANSWER) {
 84→            return getSoundList(QUESTION) + getSoundList(ANSWER)
 85→        }
 86→        return soundPaths[side] ?: emptyList()
 87→    }
 88→
 89→    /**
 90→     * Clears current sound paths; call before [expandSounds] + [addSounds]
 91→     * is called for the next card
 92→     */
 93→    fun resetSounds() {
 94→        soundPaths.clear()
 95→    }
 96→
 97→    /**
 98→     * Stores entries to the filepaths for sounds, categorized as belonging to the front (question) or back (answer) of cards.
 99→     * Note that all sounds embedded in the content will be given the same base categorization of question or answer.
100→     * Additionally, the result is to be sorted by the order of appearance on the card.
101→     * @param tags the entries expected in display order
102→     * @param side the base categorization of the sounds in the content
103→     */
104→    fun addSounds(tags: List<SoundOrVideoTag>, side: SingleSoundSide) {
105→        val soundPathCollection = soundPaths.getOrPut(side.toSoundSide()) { mutableListOf() }
106→
107→        Timber.d("Adding %d sounds to side: %s", tags.size, side)
108→        val paths = tags.map { getSoundPath(soundDir, it.filename) }
109→        soundPathCollection.addAll(paths)
110→    }
111→
112→    /** Plays all the sounds for the indicated side(s)  */
113→    fun playSounds(side: SoundSide, errorListener: OnErrorListener?) {
114→        // If there are sounds to play for the current card, start with the first one
115→        val soundPaths = getSounds(side) ?: return
116→        Timber.d("playSounds: playing $side")
117→        this.playSound(
118→            soundPaths[0],
119→            PlayAllCompletionListener(side, errorListener),
120→            errorListener
121→        )
122→    }
123→
124→    /** Returns the total length of all sounds for the side in milliseconds */
125→    fun getSoundsLength(side: SoundSide): Long {
126→        val soundPaths = getSounds(side) ?: return 0
127→        val metaRetriever = MediaMetadataRetriever()
128→        val context = AnkiDroidApp.instance.applicationContext
129→        return soundPaths
130→            .map { Uri.parse(it) }
131→            .sumOf { uri ->
132→                try {
133→                    metaRetriever.getDuration(context, uri)
134→                } catch (e: Exception) {
135→                    Timber.w(
136→                        e,
137→                        "metaRetriever - Error setting Data Source for mediaRetriever (media doesn't exist or forbidden?)."
138→                    )
139→                    0
140→                }
141→            }
142→    }
143→
144→    /**
145→     * Class used to play all sounds for a given card side
146→     */
147→    private inner class PlayAllCompletionListener(
148→        private val side: SoundSide,
149→        private val errorListener: OnErrorListener?
150→    ) : OnCompletionListener {
151→        /** Index of next sound to play inside `getSounds(side)` */
152→        // this is a completion listener: [onCompletion] is first called after the first sound
153→        private var nextIndexToPlay = 1
154→        override fun onCompletion(mp: MediaPlayer) {
155→            val paths = getSounds(side) ?: emptyList() // emptyList -> stopSounds()
156→            // If there are still more sounds to play for the current card, play the next one
157→            if (nextIndexToPlay < paths.size) {
158→                Timber.i("Play all: Playing next sound")
159→                playSound(paths[nextIndexToPlay++], this, errorListener)
160→            } else {
161→                Timber.i("Play all: Completed - releasing sound")
162→                soundPlayer.stopSounds()
163→            }
164→        }
165→    }
166→
167→    override fun stopSounds() {
168→        soundPlayer.stopSounds()
169→        ReadText.stopTts() // TODO: Reconsider design
170→    }
171→
172→    fun hasQuestion(): Boolean = getSounds(QUESTION) != null
173→
174→    fun hasAnswer(): Boolean = getSounds(ANSWER) != null
175→
176→    fun interface OnErrorListener {
177→        fun onError(mp: MediaPlayer?, which: Int, extra: Int, path: String?): ErrorHandling
178→
179→        enum class ErrorHandling {
180→            /** Stop playing audio */
181→            STOP_AUDIO,
182→
183→            /** Continue to the next audio (if any) */
184→            CONTINUE_AUDIO,
185→
186→            /** Retry the current audio */
187→            RETRY_AUDIO
188→        }
189→    }
190→
191→    companion object {
192→        /**
193→         * Pattern used to identify the markers for sound files
194→         */
195→        val SOUND_PATTERN: Pattern = Pattern.compile("\\[sound:([^\\[\\]]*)]")
196→
197→        // TODO join with SOUND_PATTERN
198→        val SOUND_RE = SOUND_PATTERN.toRegex()
199→
200→        /**
201→         * Pattern used to parse URI (according to http://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc3986#page-50)
202→         */
203→        private val sUriPattern =
204→            Pattern.compile("^(([^:/?#]+):)?(//([^/?#]*))?([^?#]*)(\\?([^#]*))?(#(.*))?$")
205→
206→        /** Extract SoundOrVideoTag instances from content where sound tags are in the form: [sound:filename.mp3]  */
207→        @CheckResult
208→        fun extractTagsFromLegacyContent(content: String): List<SoundOrVideoTag> {
209→            val matcher = SOUND_PATTERN.matcher(content)
210→            // While there is matches of the pattern for sound markers
211→            val ret = mutableListOf<SoundOrVideoTag>()
212→            while (matcher.find()) {
213→                // Get the sound file name
214→                val sound = matcher.group(1)!!
215→                ret.add(SoundOrVideoTag(sound))
216→            }
217→            return ret
218→        }
219→
220→        /**
221→         * expandSounds takes content with embedded sound file placeholders and expands them to reference the actual media
222→         * file
223→         *
224→         * @param content -- card content to be rendered that may contain embedded audio
225→         * @return -- the same content but in a format that will render working play buttons when audio was embedded
226→         */
227→        fun expandSounds(content: String): String {
228→            return addPlayIcons(content)
229→        }
230→
231→        /**
232→         * @param soundDir -- base path to the media files.
233→         * @param sound -- path to the sound file from the card content.
234→         * @return absolute URI to the sound file.
235→         */
236→        fun getSoundPath(soundDir: String, sound: String): String {
237→            val trimmedSound = sound.trim { it <= ' ' }
238→            return if (hasURIScheme(trimmedSound)) {
239→                trimmedSound
240→            } else {
241→                soundDir + Uri.encode(sound.trimEnd())
242→            }
243→        }
244→
245→        /**
246→         * @param path -- path to the sound file from the card content.
247→         * @return true if path is well-formed URI and contains URI scheme.
248→         */
249→        private fun hasURIScheme(path: String): Boolean {
250→            val uriMatcher = sUriPattern.matcher(path.trim { it <= ' ' })
251→            return uriMatcher.matches() && uriMatcher.group(2) != null
252→        }
253→    }
254→}
255→
256→open class SoundPlayer {
257→    val currentAudioUri: String?
258→        get() = mCurrentAudioUri?.toString()
259→
260→    val isCurrentAudioFinished: Boolean
261→        get() = mMediaPlayer == null
262→
263→    /**

387→                    Sound.OnErrorListener.ErrorHandling.RETRY_AUDIO -> playMedia()

392→        playMedia()

 31→
 32→class HtmlGenerator(
 33→    private val typeAnswer: TypeAnswer,
 34→    val cardAppearance: CardAppearance,
 35→    val cardTemplate: CardTemplate,
 36→    val resources: Resources
 37→) {
 38→
 39→    @CheckResult
 40→    fun generateHtml(card: Card, reload: Boolean, side: Side): CardHtml {
 41→        return CardHtml.createInstance(card, reload, side, this)
 42→    }
 43→
 44→    fun filterTypeAnswer(content: String, side: Side): String {
 45→        return when (side) {
 46→            Side.FRONT -> typeAnswer.filterQuestion(content)
 47→            Side.BACK -> typeAnswer.filterAnswer(content)
 48→        }
 49→    }
 50→
 51→    fun expandSounds(content: String): String {
 52→        return Sound.expandSounds(content)
 53→    }
 54→
 55→    companion object {
 56→        fun createInstance(
 57→            context: Context,
 58→            typeAnswer: TypeAnswer
 59→        ): HtmlGenerator {
 60→            val preferences = context.sharedPrefs()
 61→            val cardAppearance = CardAppearance.create(ReviewerCustomFonts(), preferences)
 62→            val cardHtmlTemplate = loadCardTemplate(context)
 63→
 64→            return HtmlGenerator(
 65→                typeAnswer,
 66→                cardAppearance,
 67→                cardHtmlTemplate,
 68→                context.resources
 69→            )
 70→        }
 71→
 72→        /**
 73→         * Load the template for the card
 74→         */
 75→        private fun loadCardTemplate(viewer: Context): CardTemplate {
 76→            try {
 77→                val data = convertStreamToString(viewer.assets.open("card_template.html"))
 78→                return CardTemplate(data)
 79→            } catch (e: IOException) {
 80→                Timber.w(e)
 81→                throw RuntimeException(e)
 82→            }
 83→        }
 84→
 85→        /**
 86→         * Converts an InputStream to a String.
 87→         *
 88→         * @param input InputStream to convert
 89→         * @return String version of the InputStream
 90→         */
 91→        private fun convertStreamToString(input: InputStream?): String {
 92→            var contentOfMyInputStream = ""
 93→            try {
 94→                val rd = BufferedReader(InputStreamReader(input), 4096)
 95→                var line: String?
 96→                val sb = StringBuilder()
 97→                while (rd.readLine().also { line = it } != null) {
 98→                    sb.append(line)
 99→                }
100→                rd.close()
101→                contentOfMyInputStream = sb.toString()
102→            } catch (e: Exception) {
103→                Timber.w(e)
104→            }
105→            return contentOfMyInputStream
106→        }
107→    }
108→}

25→fun SharedPreferences.get(key: String): Any? {

29→/**
30→ * Sets the callback to be invoked when this preference is changed by the user
31→ * (but before the internal state has been updated) on the internal onPreferenceChangeListener,
32→ * returning true on it by default
33→ * @param onPreferenceChangeListener The callback to be invoked
34→ */
35→fun Preference.setOnPreferenceChangeListener(onPreferenceChangeListener: (newValue: Any) -> Unit) {
36→    this.setOnPreferenceChangeListener { _, newValue ->
37→        onPreferenceChangeListener(newValue)
38→        true
39→    }

42→/** Obtains a non-null reference to the preference defined by the key, or throws  */
43→inline fun <reified T : Preference> PreferenceFragmentCompat.requirePreference(key: String): T {
44→    val preference = findPreference<Preference>(key)
45→        ?: throw IllegalStateException("missing preference: '$key'")
46→    return preference as T
47→}
48→
49→/**
50→ * Obtains a non-null reference to the preference whose
51→ * key is defined with given [resId] or throws
52→ * e.g. `requirePreference(R.string.day_theme_key)` returns
53→ * the preference whose key is `@string/day_theme_key`
54→ * The resource IDs with preferences keys can be found on `res/values/preferences.xml`
55→ */
56→inline fun <reified T : Preference> PreferenceFragmentCompat.requirePreference(@StringRes resId: Int): T {
57→    val key = getString(resId)
58→    return requirePreference(key)
59→}

61→/** shorthand method to get the default [SharedPreferences] instance */
62→fun Context.sharedPrefs(): SharedPreferences {
